Share of Aggregate Income by Percentile
Share of Aggregate Income by Percentile
*An asterisk preceding an estimate indicates change is statistically different from zero at the 90 percent confidence level.

1 A margin of error (MOE) is a measure of an estimate's variability. The larger the MOE in relation to the size of the estimate, the less reliable the estimate. This number, when added to and subtracted from the estimate, forms the 90 percent confidence interval. MOEs shown in this table are based on standard errors calculated using replicate weights.
2 Calculated estimate may be different due to rounded components.
Note: Median income estimates are calculated using linear interpolation and $2,500 intervals.
Source: U.S. Census Bureau, Current Population Survey, 2024 and 2025 Annual Social and Economic Supplements (CPS ASEC; DMS number P-7534374, DRB approval number CBDRB-FY25-0384).
